question:
Find, with explanation, the maximum value of $f(x)=x^3-3x$ on the
set of all real numbers $x$ satisfying $x^4+36\leq 13x^2$.
solution: Solution. The condition \( x^{4}+36 \leq 13 x^{2} \) is equivalent to \( (x-3)(x-2)(x+2)(x+3) \leq \) 0 , which is satisfied if and only if \( x \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3] \). The function \( f \) is increasing on \( [-3,-2] \) and on \( [2,3] \), since \( f^{\prime}(x)=3\left(x^{2}-1\right)>0 \) on these intervals. Hence the maximum value is \( \max \{f(-2), f(3)\}=18 \).

descriptive_long.question:
Find, with explanation, the maximum value of $function(variable)=variable^3-3variable$ on the
set of all real numbers $variable$ satisfying $variable^4+36\leq 13variable^2$.
descriptive_long.solution: Solution. The condition \( variable^{4}+36 \leq 13 variable^{2} \) is equivalent to \( (variable-3)(variable-2)(variable+2)(variable+3) \leq \) 0 , which is satisfied if and only if \( variable \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3] \). The function \( function \) is increasing on \( [-3,-2] \) and on \( [2,3] \), since \( function^{\prime}(variable)=3\left(variable^{2}-1\right)>0 \) on these intervals. Hence the maximum value is \( \max \{function(-2), function(3)\}=18 \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Find, with explanation, the maximum value of $porcupine(tangerine)=tangerine^3-3tangerine$ on the
set of all real numbers $tangerine$ satisfying $tangerine^4+36\leq 13tangerine^2$.
descriptive_long_confusing.solution: Solution. The condition \( tangerine^{4}+36 \leq 13 tangerine^{2} \) is equivalent to \( (tangerine-3)(tangerine-2)(tangerine+2)(tangerine+3) \leq \) 0 , which is satisfied if and only if \( tangerine \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3] \). The function \( porcupine \) is increasing on \( [-3,-2] \) and on \( [2,3] \), since \( porcupine^{\prime}(tangerine)=3\left(tangerine^{2}-1\right)>0 \) on these intervals. Hence the maximum value is \( \max \{porcupine(-2), porcupine(3)\}=18 \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Find, with explanation, the maximum value of $nonfunction(knownvalue)=knownvalue^3-3knownvalue$ on the
set of all real numbers $knownvalue$ satisfying $knownvalue^4+36\leq 13knownvalue^2$.
descriptive_long_misleading.solution: Solution. The condition \( knownvalue^{4}+36 \leq 13 knownvalue^{2} \) is equivalent to \( (knownvalue-3)(knownvalue-2)(knownvalue+2)(knownvalue+3) \leq \) 0 , which is satisfied if and only if \( knownvalue \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3] \). The function \( nonfunction \) is increasing on \( [-3,-2] \) and on \( [2,3] \), since \( nonfunction^{\prime}(knownvalue)=3\left(knownvalue^{2}-1\right)>0 \) on these intervals. Hence the maximum value is \( \max \{nonfunction(-2), nonfunction(3)\}=18 \).

garbled_string.question:
Find, with explanation, the maximum value of $hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp)=qzxwvtnp^3-3qzxwvtnp$ on the
set of all real numbers $qzxwvtnp$ satisfying $qzxwvtnp^4+36\leq 13qzxwvtnp^2$.
garbled_string.solution: Solution. The condition \( qzxwvtnp^{4}+36 \leq 13 qzxwvtnp^{2} \) is equivalent to \( (qzxwvtnp-3)(qzxwvtnp-2)(qzxwvtnp+2)(qzxwvtnp+3) \leq \) 0 , which is satisfied if and only if \( qzxwvtnp \in[-3,-2] \cup[2,3] \). The function \( hjgrksla \) is increasing on \( [-3,-2] \) and on \( [2,3] \), since \( hjgrksla^{\prime}(qzxwvtnp)=3\left(qzxwvtnp^{2}-1\right)>0 \) on these intervals. Hence the maximum value is \( \max \{hjgrksla(-2), hjgrksla(3)\}=18 \).

kernel_variant.question:
Determine, with proof, the maximum value of the function
\[
\displaystyle f(x)=x^{3}-2x
\]
over all real numbers \(x\) that satisfy the inequality
\[
 x^{4}+25\;\le\;26x^{2}.
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
First rewrite the constraint in a factored form.  
\[  
 x^{4}+25\le 26x^{2}\;\iff\;x^{4}-26x^{2}+25\le 0.  
\]  
Observe that  
\[  
 x^{4}-26x^{2}+25=(x^{2}-25)(x^{2}-1)=(x-5)(x-1)(x+1)(x+5).  
\]  
Hence the inequality is satisfied precisely when the product of the four linear factors is non-positive, that is,  
\[  
 x\in[-5,-1]\;\cup\;[1,5].\tag{1}  
\]
Next examine the monotonicity of the objective function on each of the two admissible intervals.  
\[  
 f'(x)=3x^{2}-2.  
\]  
The derivative vanishes when \(3x^{2}-2=0\), i.e. at \(x=\pm\sqrt{\tfrac23}\approx\pm0.816\).  These critical points lie strictly between \(-1\) and \(1\).  Consequently,  
\[  
 f'(x)=3x^{2}-2>0\quad\text{for}\quad |x|\ge1.  
\]  
In particular, \(f'\) is positive on each of the admissible intervals (1).  Therefore \(f\) is strictly increasing on both \([-5,-1]\) and on \([1,5]\).

Because \(f\) is increasing on each interval, its maximum over the entire feasible set occurs at the right-hand endpoint of each interval.  Thus  
\[  
 f(-1)=(-1)^{3}-2(-1)=-1+2=1,\qquad f(5)=5^{3}-2\cdot5=125-10=115.  
\]  
Comparing the two values, the larger is 115.

Hence the maximum value of \(f(x)=x^{3}-2x\) subject to \(x^{4}+25\le26x^{2}\) is  
\[  
\boxed{115}.  
\]

core_steps: Rewrite the constraint as a factored quartic (difference-of-squares) to get two closed intervals for x
Differentiate f and check the sign of f′ on each admissible interval to see that f is increasing there
Conclude that the maximum of f occurs at the right-hand endpoint of each interval and evaluate f at those endpoints

slot1.description: The two positive numbers whose squares give the four real roots of the quartic, hence the endpoints of the admissible intervals

slot2.description: The coefficient of x in f(x)=x³−c x; it controls where f′(x)=3x²−c vanishes, which must be inside (−β,β) so that f′ keeps one sign on each admissible interval
